name: Circular gravel on the Transpirenaica
description:
I propose a Route that combines sections of the Transpyrenean with others of the Pedals de Foc through a route that you will not forget.

If you have not already done the entire “Transpi” trip, with this itinerary you are going to try one of its most spectacular segments and you will have no choice but to plan a pedal vacation through the Pyrenees to cover the rest of the stages.

If you already know the area, you will know that you have ahead of you what for many are the most impressive landscapes in the whole of the Pyrenees.

83 kilometers and more than 2,000 meters of unevenness await you in a tough route, although without technical complications. Magnificent tracks and a bit of road to enjoy a day of authentic Pyrenean gravel.

The beginning and end of the route is the Sort bus station, with direct lines to Lleida and Barcelona, in case you decide to arrive by public transport.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Sort offers challenging routes through the mountainous terrain of the Pyrenees. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, with trails often leading through high passes and offering expansive views. These routes frequently traverse varied surfaces, including forest tracks and mountain roads. The landscape provides a demanding environment suitable for experienced gravel bikers. Best gravel bike trails around Sort The most popular gravel bike trail is Circular gravel on the Transpirenaica, a 52.0 miles (83.6 km) trail that takes 8 hours 13 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial climbs and descents across the Trans-Pyrenean landscape. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Hermitage of Sant Joan de l'Erm – Refugi de la Basseta loop from Sant Joan de l'Erm, a difficult 32.1 miles (51.6 km) path. This route explores high mountain areas, passing by historical hermitage sites and mountain refuges. Local gravel bikers also love the Rialp – Triador Pass and Viewpoint loop from Rialp, a 31.9 miles (51.4 km) trail leading through mountain passes and offering panoramic viewpoints, often completed in about 5 hours 23 minutes. Gravel biking around Sort is defined by high mountain passes, challenging ascents, and extensive forest tracks. The network offers options primarily for experienced gravel bikers seeking demanding routes. The routes in Sort are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. More than 60 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Sort's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of gravel biking trails around Sort?
A: Gravel biking around Sort is characterized by challenging routes through the mountainous terrain of the Pyrenees. All 10 available routes are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation changes, high passes, and varied surfaces like forest tracks and mountain roads. These trails are best suited for experienced gravel bikers.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easy gravel bike routes in Sort?
A: The gravel biking routes in Sort are primarily designed for experienced riders due to the challenging mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes. There are no routes currently rated as easy or moderate; all 10 available routes are classified as difficult. Therefore, they may not be suitable for families with young children or beginners.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on gravel bike trails near Sort?
A: The trails around Sort offer stunning high mountain landscapes, often leading through high passes and providing expansive views of the Pyrenees. You'll encounter extensive forest tracks and mountain roads. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rialp – Triador Pass and Viewpoint loop from Rialp&lt;/a&gt; is known for its panoramic viewpoints.

Q: Are there circular gravel bike routes available in Sort?
A: Yes, many of the gravel bike routes around Sort are circular. For instance, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e825122758/circular-gravel-on-the-transpirenaica" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular gravel on the Transpirenaica&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23743276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Sant Joan de l'Erm – Refugi de la Basseta loop from Sant Joan de l'Erm&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31489129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plans de Llessuí – View of Bago de Llessuí loop from Llessui&lt;/a&gt;, all of which are loops.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of gravel bike routes in Sort?
A: The gravel bike routes in Sort vary in length and duration, reflecting their challenging nature. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e825122758/circular-gravel-on-the-transpirenaica" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular gravel on the Transpirenaica&lt;/a&gt; is 83.6 km (52.0 miles) and takes approximately 8 hours 13 minutes. Shorter options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espot Village Center – Espot loop from Espot&lt;/a&gt; are around 23.1 km (14.4 miles) and can be completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes.

Q: What do other gravel bikers say about the trails in Sort?
A: The gravel biking routes in Sort are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 4 reviews. Over 60 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore the varied and demanding terrain, often praising the challenging ascents and the stunning high mountain passes.

Q: Are there any notable natural attractions or landmarks along the gravel bike trails near Sort?
A: Yes, the region around Sort is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You might encounter stunning lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Sant Maurici&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/197506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ratera Lake&lt;/a&gt;, or the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1033042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ratera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. There are also mountain refuges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/567109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Josep Maria Blanc Refuge&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amitges Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, which can be points of interest on longer tours.

Q: What kind of mountain passes can I expect to encounter while gravel biking in Sort?
A: The gravel biking experience in Sort is defined by its high mountain passes. You can expect to climb significant passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/762064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port del Cantó (1,721 m)&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3387939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Triador&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4349421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada de Saburó&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Oli&lt;/a&gt;. These passes contribute to the challenging nature and rewarding views of the routes.

Q: What is the best season for gravel biking in Sort?
A: Given the high mountain passes and significant elevation, the best season for gravel biking in Sort is typically from late spring to early autumn (roughly May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and trails are less likely to be affected by snow or ice, which can make routes impassable or dangerous in winter.

Q: Are there any towns or villages near the gravel biking routes where I can find amenities?
A: Yes, several routes start or pass near charming towns and villages in the region. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rialp – Triador Pass and Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt; starts from Rialp, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espot Village Center – Espot loop&lt;/a&gt; begins in Espot. These villages often offer basic amenities like cafes, restaurants, and accommodation.

Q: How many gravel bike routes are available in the Sort region?
A: There are 10 dedicated gravel bike routes available in the Sort region, all of which are classified as difficult due to the mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes.
tips: Take advantage and refill water and food: if you are following the Trans-Pyrenees, it is the last inhabited point for many kilometers.
